etymology.description: 冃 ('covering') -> phonetic 冒 + semantic 巾 ('cloth') added -> 帽
etymology.interpretation: The character 帽 originally depicted a covering with the element 冃. By adding 冒 (mào), the phonetic element, and 巾 (jīn, 'cloth'), the semantic component, it specified something that covers the head made of cloth, which logically leads to the meaning 'hat'.

examples.chinese: 他戴了一顶红色的帽子(他戴了一頂紅色的帽子)。
examples.pinyin: Tā dài le yī dǐng hóngsè de màozi.

examples.chinese: 这顶帽子是我去年买的(這頂帽子是我去年買的)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè dǐng màozi shì wǒ qùnián mǎi de.
examples.english: This hat is the one I bought last year.

examples.chinese: 她在商店看见了一顶漂亮的帽子(她在商店看見了一頂漂亮的帽子)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài shāngdiàn kànjiàn le yī dǐng piàoliang de màozi.
examples.english: She saw a beautiful hat at the store.
